apps.name: Cloudflare
apps.description: Manage Cloudflare configuration as code through the Cloudflare API (v4). Covers DNS, the WAF/firewall Rulesets engine (custom rules, rate limiting, redirects, transforms and managed-ruleset overrides), zone settings, account Lists, and Zero Trust (Access applications/policies/groups/service-tokens and Gateway rules/lists) — with validation, drift detection and rollback handled by the Security-as-Code pipeline. The component hostname is the zone domain; the app resolves zone and account ids automatically.
apps.releaseNotes:
## 1.1.0 — 2026-07-20

### Changed
- Grouped the **Configurations** sidebar into 5 collapsible sections — Zone, WAF
  & Security, Rules & Lists, Zero Trust · Access, and Zero Trust · Gateway — so
  all 14 configuration types stay navigable. Sections collapse by default,
  remember whether you left them open, and always expand the one you're
  currently working in.

apps.name: Cortex XSOAR
apps.description: Manage Palo Alto Networks Cortex XSOAR content configuration as code through the XSOAR server REST API. Covers lists, incident types and scheduled jobs — each reconciled by name with validation, drift detection and rollback handled by the Security-as-Code pipeline. Works with Cortex XSOAR 6.x (API-key header) and Cortex XSOAR 8 / the Cortex platform (API key + x-xdr-auth-id via the API gateway).

apps.name: CrowdStrike Falcon
apps.description: Manage CrowdStrike Falcon configurations as code through the Falcon APIs. Includes host groups, prevention policies, and custom IOCs (indicators of compromise), with validation, drift detection, and rollback handled by the Security-as-Code pipeline.

apps.name: CyberArk Privileged Access Manager
apps.description: Manage CyberArk Privileged Access Manager (PVWA) configuration as code through the Privileged Access Security Web Services REST API. Covers safes, safe members (authorizations) and privileged accounts — with validation, drift detection and rollback handled by the Security-as-Code pipeline. The component hostname is the PVWA web server; auth is the PVWA logon flow (a manager username + password, method CyberArk / LDAP / RADIUS) that returns a session token.

apps.name: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint
apps.description: Manage Microsoft Defender for Endpoint threat intelligence as code. Covers file, network and certificate indicators (IoCs) via the Defender /api/indicators API, plus custom detection rules (PREVIEW) via the Microsoft Graph beta API — with validation, drift detection and rollback handled by the Security-as-Code pipeline. Auth is an Azure AD app registration (OAuth2 client credentials); the component hostname is the Defender API host (api.security.microsoft.com) and the tenant is set via the Tenant ID app setting.

apps.name: Elastic Security
apps.description: Manage Elastic Security configuration as code through the Kibana and Elasticsearch APIs. Covers detection rules, exception lists, index lifecycle (ILM) policies, role mappings and Kibana spaces, with validation, drift detection and rollback handled by the Security-as-Code pipeline.

apps.name: HashiCorp Vault
apps.description: Manage HashiCorp Vault configuration as code through the Vault HTTP API. Covers ACL policies (HCL), auth methods, secret engine mounts, and audit devices, with validation, drift detection and rollback handled by the Security-as-Code pipeline.
apps.releaseNotes:
## 1.3.0 — 2026-07-20

### Changed
- Grouped the **Configurations** sidebar into 5 collapsible sections — Policies,
  Authentication, Secrets, Operations, and Identity — so all 11 configuration
  types stay navigable. Sections collapse by default, remember whether you left
  them open, and always expand the one you're currently working in.

apps.name: Microsoft Intune
apps.description: Manage Microsoft Intune endpoint-security policies as code through the Microsoft Graph API. v1.0 ships Attack Surface Reduction (ASR) rules — the finite, well-defined Defender hardening rule set — with validation, drift detection and rollback handled by the Security-as-Code pipeline. These policies configure Microsoft Defender for Endpoint and can be delivered via Intune MDM or Defender security settings management. Auth is an Azure AD app registration (OAuth2 client credentials) with the Graph DeviceManagementConfiguration permission; the tenant is set via the Tenant ID app setting.

apps.name: Microsoft Sentinel
apps.description: Manage Microsoft Sentinel detection and response content as code through the Azure Resource Manager (ARM) API. v1.0 ships the genuinely CRUD, generally available configuration surface: scheduled (KQL) analytics rules, automation rules, and watchlists — each with validation, drift detection and rollback handled by the Security-as-Code pipeline. Auth is an Azure Entra app registration (OAuth2 client credentials) with the "Microsoft Sentinel Contributor" role scoped to the workspace resource group. The workspace is addressed via the Tenant ID, Subscription ID, Resource Group and Workspace Name app settings. NRT/ML/Threat-Intelligence analytics rule kinds, threat-intel indicator upload, and data connectors are preview or portal-heavy and are out of scope.

apps.name: Okta
apps.description: Manage Okta configuration as code through the Okta Management API. Covers policies and their rules, group rules, groups, network zones, trusted origins, behavior rules, authenticators, identity providers, event / inline hooks, authorization servers (scopes / claims / policies), applications and group assignments, plus security and IAM governance — ThreatInsight, log streams, device assurance policies, user types, custom admin roles with resource sets and their role bindings, custom profile-schema attributes and profile mappings, self-service feature toggles, CAPTCHA, user linked-object definitions, org rate-limit settings, brands and their themes, custom email domains and SMS templates — with validation, drift detection and rollback handled by the Security-as-Code pipeline.
apps.releaseNotes:
## 1.7.1 — 2026-07-20

### Fixed
- Saving a Connection now also registers its **deploy target** (an `okta-org`
  component whose hostname is the connection's endpoint, linked to the
  credential and environment). Previously a connection created only a credential,
  so Deploy stayed disabled ("register an okta-org connection to deploy") even
  though the connection tested green. Re-saving an existing connection back-fills
  its target. Requires app-sdk ≥ 3.3.0.

apps.name: Palo Alto Panorama
apps.description: Manage Palo Alto Networks Panorama / PAN-OS configuration as code through the PAN-OS REST API. Covers tags, address objects, service objects, address groups, service groups and security pre-rules — with validation, drift detection and rollback handled by the Security-as-Code pipeline. Writes land in the Panorama candidate config and are committed to Panorama (XML API) when auto-commit is on. The component hostname is the Panorama management host; auth is a pre-generated PAN-OS API key sent as the X-PAN-KEY header.

apps.name: Proofpoint Essentials
apps.description: Manage Proofpoint Essentials email security configuration as code through the Essentials Interface API (v1). Covers protected domains and their inbound mail routing, plus the organization Safe and Blocked sender lists — with validation, drift detection and rollback handled by the Security-as-Code pipeline. Objects are scoped to the organization named in the Organization (primary domain) setting.

apps.name: Qualys
apps.description: Manage Qualys VMDR / Policy Compliance configuration as code through the classic v2 API. Covers asset groups, static search lists (QID sets) and scheduled VM scans — with validation, drift detection and rollback handled by the Security-as-Code pipeline. The component hostname is your Qualys platform API server (Help > About); auth is HTTP Basic with the required X-Requested-With header.

apps.name: Rapid7 InsightVM
apps.description: Manage Rapid7 InsightVM configuration as code through the Security Console API (v3). Covers scan sites, asset groups, tags, scan templates, scan engine pools, shared and site credentials, scan schedules and vulnerability exceptions — with validation, drift detection and rollback handled by the Security-as-Code pipeline. The component hostname is the console host:3780; auth is HTTP Basic.
apps.releaseNotes:
## 1.1.0 — 2026-07-20

### Changed
- Grouped the **Configurations** sidebar into 4 collapsible sections — Scanning,
  Assets, Credentials, and Findings — so all 9 configuration types stay
  navigable. Sections collapse by default, remember whether you left them open,
  and always expand the one you're currently working in.

apps.name: SentinelOne
apps.description: Manage SentinelOne endpoint security configuration as code through the SentinelOne Management API (v2.1). Covers exclusions, blocklist and allowlist hashes, STAR custom detection rules, the per-scope agent policy, and agent groups — with validation, drift detection and rollback handled by the Security-as-Code pipeline. Objects are scoped across the Account/Site/Group hierarchy via the Scope + Scope ID settings.

apps.name: Snyk
apps.description: Manage Snyk organization configuration as code across the Snyk REST and v1 APIs. Covers Snyk Code (SAST) enablement, notification preferences, SCM integration pull-request and auto-upgrade settings, automation service accounts and outbound webhooks — with validation, drift detection and rollback handled by the Security-as-Code pipeline. Auth is a Snyk API token (a service-account token is recommended); the component hostname is the Snyk region API host (api.snyk.io, api.eu.snyk.io, …) and most configuration is scoped to the Organization ID app setting.

apps.name: Splunk Cloud Platform
apps.description: Manage Splunk Cloud Platform configurations as code through the Admin Config Service (ACS) API. Includes index management, HTTP Event Collector (HEC) tokens, IP allow lists, and private apps/add-ons (built to a .spl, vetted by AppInspect, installed through ACS). Roles are managed through the Splunk Cloud Platform REST API on the stack's management port 8089 — ACS cannot manage identity.
apps.releaseNotes:
## 1.9.0 — 2026-07-20

### Changed
- Grouped the **Configurations** sidebar into 5 collapsible sections — Data,
  Network & Access, System Settings, Apps, and Access Control — so all 13
  configuration types stay navigable. Sections collapse by default, remember
  whether you left them open, and always expand the one you're currently
  working in.

apps.name: Splunk Enterprise
apps.description: Manage Splunk Enterprise configurations as code. Author Splunk apps and add-ons (including their .conf files), HTTP Event Collector tokens, BYOL infrastructure, and version tracking for current Splunk release lines (9.4.x–10.4.x). Configuration ships the way Splunk ships it — inside an app — so indexes and roles are authored as indexes.conf / authorize.conf in the App Contents of a Splunk App.

apps.name: Splunk SOAR
apps.description: Connect Veltrix to Splunk SOAR (Security Orchestration, Automation and Response). Manages the SOAR instance connection — API endpoint (component), credential, TLS verification, request timeout, retries, and optional proxy — so the platform can reach your SOAR deployment.

apps.name: Tenable Vulnerability Management
apps.description: Manage Tenable Vulnerability Management (tenable.io) configuration as code through the Tenable VM REST API. Covers scheduled scans, asset tags, and scan exclusions (blackout windows), with validation, drift detection and rollback handled by the Security-as-Code pipeline.
apps.releaseNotes:
## 1.2.0 — 2026-07-20

### Changed
- Grouped the **Configurations** sidebar into 7 collapsible sections — Scanning,
  Assets, Credentials & Connectors, Organization, Agents, Findings, and Access
  Control — so all 18 configuration types stay navigable. Sections collapse by
  default, remember whether you left them open, and always expand the one you're
  currently working in.

apps.name: Wiz
apps.description: Manage Wiz (CNAPP / cloud security) configuration as code through the Wiz GraphQL API. Covers service accounts (createServiceAccount — the generated client secret is a write-only output and is never captured) and custom cloud configuration (CSPM) rules — with validation, drift detection and rollback handled by the Security-as-Code pipeline. Authenticates as a Wiz service account via OAuth2 client credentials against the tenant's regional API host.

apps.name: Zscaler
apps.description: Manage Zscaler Internet Access (ZIA) and Zscaler Private Access (ZPA) configuration as code through the Zscaler OneAPI. Covers URL filtering, cloud firewall, DLP, SSL inspection, locations and admin config on ZIA, and application segments, server/segment groups, connectors and access policy on ZPA - with validation, drift detection and rollback handled by the Security-as-Code pipeline. ZIA changes are staged and activated as a batch; ZPA changes apply immediately.
apps.releaseNotes:
## 1.2.0 — 2026-07-20

### Changed
- Grouped the **Configurations** sidebar into 7 collapsible sections split by
  service — ZIA (Policy Rules, Objects & Groups, DLP, Traffic Forwarding,
  Administration) and ZPA (Infrastructure, Applications & Policy) — so all 32
  configuration types stay navigable. Sections collapse by default, remember
  whether you left them open, and always expand the one you're currently
  working in.
